description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant with a cylindrical body and a domed top, featuring a prominent central stem and two side outlets capped with round covers. The hydrant is constructed from heavy cast metal, with visible bolts around the base and a weathered, chipped red paint finish that reveals some metallic wear. Two short chains are attached to the side caps, likely to prevent the caps from being lost when removed. The top of the hydrant has a raised operating nut or cap, used for opening the water valve. The surface is rough and textured, showing signs of exposure to the elements, such as rust and scuff marks. No manufacturer or model markings are visible on the exterior.
description: A weathered red fire hydrant with two chained side caps, a domed top, and a rugged, chipped surface. The hydrant is made of heavy metal and shows signs of outdoor wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the chained cap facing forward and the central operating nut on top.
description_view_1: Rear view with both side caps and chains visible, and the back of the cylindrical body exposed.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting one of the chained caps and the textured, worn surface of the hydrant.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the other chained cap and a clear look at the base bolts and weathered paint.
